preview_text:
Panama is the model's heavy favorite, with a 67% win probability against Ghana's 9.7%. The underdog faces a significant gap, though a draw is plausible at 23%, leaving outcome uncertainty distributed unevenly across the three possible results.

The model sees this as a moderately competitive match in terms of draw likelihood, but the win probability spread tilts decisively toward Panama. Ghana's path to victory is narrow; Panama's advantage is substantial but not extreme enough to make draws implausible or to suggest a rout is the modal outcome.

The market diverges sharply from the model. Sportsbooks assign Ghana a 49% win probability—nearly 40 percentage points higher than the model—while pricing Panama at just 24%, roughly 43 points lower. Draw probabilities are close (model 23%, market 26%). The dashboard does not adjudicate which source is correct; the discrepancy is large enough to warrant attention from users comparing statistical and bookmaker views. No Polymarket per-match consensus is available yet.
